task_completion.description: Open OpenRocket and load the initial simple_model_rocket.ork file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket GUI is visible with 'simple_model_rocket' loaded in the main window.

task_completion.description: Add a 'Mass Component' named 'Tuning Ballast' inside the nose cone
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rocket design tree shows a 'Mass Component' (ideally renamed 'Tuning Ballast') as a child node under the 'Nose cone'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the rocket uses an Estes C6-5 motor
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Motors & Configuration' tab in OpenRocket shows an Estes C6-5 (or C6) motor assigned to the rocket.

task_completion.description: Adjust the ballast mass to achieve a simulated apogee of 200.0m ± 2.0m
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight Simulations' tab shows a completed simulation run with an Apogee value between 198.0m and 202.0m.

task_completion.description: Save the modified rocket design to tuned_altitude_rocket.ork
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or the OpenRocket window title indicates the file is being saved as 'tuned_altitude_rocket.ork' in the /home/ga/Documents/rockets/ directory.

task_completion.description: Create a tuning report containing the motor used, final ballast mass, and final simulated apogee
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the creation of 'ballast_tuning_report.txt' in /home/ga/Documents/exports/ containing the motor name (C6), the specific ballast mass used, and the final apogee (~200m).

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the rocket and run simulations
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with OpenRocket dialog boxes (e.g., Mass Component configuration, Flight Simulations tab) rather than directly editing the .ork XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The apogee result comes from an actual OpenRocket simulation
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket 'Flight Simulations' tab displays a recently run simulation with the target apogee, showing that the agent actually clicked 'Run simulations'.

integrity.description: The tuning report contains specific, derived values rather than generic placeholders
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file contains a specific ballast mass value (e.g., '14.5g') that matches the mass configured in the OpenRocket GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target output files: '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/tuned_altitude_rocket.ork' and '/home/ga/Documents/exports/ballast_tuning_report.txt'. Target altitude is 200.0m with a tolerance of 2.0m (acceptable range: 198.0m - 202.0m). The target motor is 'C6'. The 'Tuning Ballast' must be a mass component located inside the nose cone with a mass > 0g. Note: The original file already contains a C6-5 motor, so the agent may not need to replace it, just verify or leave it.
